Definition(EN): An item definition is a kind of occurrence definition (see 7.9 ) that defines a class of identifiable objects that may be acted on over time, but which do not necessarily perform actions themselves.
Context/Example(EN): Item usages can be used to represent inputs and outputs to actions such as water, fuel, electrical signals and data. Item usages, such as fuel, may flow through a system and be stored by a system. An item may have attributes (see 7.7 ), states (see 7.18 ), and nested item usages. An item that performs actions is normally modeled as a part (see 7.11 ). All parts are items, but not all items are necessarily parts.

Definition(EN): An item usage is a usage of one or more item definitions.
Context/Example(EN): Item usages can be used to represent inputs and outputs to actions such as water, fuel, electrical signals and data.

Definition(EN): The shape of an item is its boundary in three-dimensional space.
Context/Example(EN): Items without shapes are closed enabling them to be boundaries of other items (for example, a two-dimensional sphere has no boundary, but it is the shape of a three-dimensional ball).

Definition(EN): An item can also identify other items as enveloping shapes, which are closed items that are the boundary of a hypothetical item occupying the same or larger space as the enveloped item.
Context/Example(EN): An item can also identify other items as enveloping shapes, which are closed items that are the boundary of a hypothetical item occupying the same or larger space as the enveloped item.

Definition(EN): Some of these can be bounding shapes, which overlap the bounded item on all sides.
Context/Example(EN): Some of these can be bounding shapes, which overlap the bounded item on all sides.

Definition(EN): The interior of which is a void of the item.
Context/Example(EN): Items with no voids are solid.

Definition(EN): Items with no voids are solid.
Context/Example(EN): Items with no voids are solid.

Definition(EN): An item definition or usage (that is not of a more specialized kind) is declared as a kind of occurrence definition or usage (see 7.9.2 ), using the kind keyword item.
Context/Example(EN): item def Fuel { attribute pressure : PressureValue; ref item impurities[0..*] : Material; }
